subtopics.title: Tokenization
subtopics.explanation: Tokenization breaks text into smaller units called tokens (words, subwords, characters, or sentences). Types: 1) Word tokenization: splits by spaces/punctuation, 2) Sentence tokenization: splits into sentences, 3) Subword tokenization: splits rare words into common subwords (BPE, WordPiece), 4) Character tokenization: splits into characters. Subword tokenization (used in BERT, GPT) handles out-of-vocabulary words by breaking into known subwords. Tokenization is the first step in any NLP pipeline, converting raw text into numerical IDs for model input.
subtopics.example: Text: 'I love NLP!' → Word tokens: ['I', 'love', 'NLP', '!'] → Subword tokens (BPE): ['I', 'love', 'N', 'LP', '!'] → Sentence tokens: ['I love NLP!']
subtopics.code:
import re
import nltk
from nltk.tokenize import word_tokenize, sent_tokenize
from transformers import AutoTokenizer
import spacy

# Download required data
nltk.download('punkt', quiet=True)

class TokenizationDemo:
    """Demonstrate various tokenization methods"""
    
    @staticmethod
    def word_tokenize_basic(text):
        """Basic word tokenization with regex"""
        return re.findall(r'\b\w+\b|[^\w\s]', text)
    
    @staticmethod
    def word_tokenize_nltk(text):
        """NLTK word tokenization"""
        return word_tokenize(text)
    
    @staticmethod
    def sentence_tokenize_nltk(text):
        """NLTK sentence tokenization"""
        return sent_tokenize(text)
    
    @staticmethod
    def subword_tokenize_transformers(text, model_name='bert-base-uncased'):
        """Subword tokenization using HuggingFace transformers"""
        tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_name)
        tokens = tokenizer.tokenize(text)
        token_ids = tokenizer.encode(text, add_special_tokens=True)
        return tokens, token_ids
    
    @staticmethod
    def subword_tokenize_bert(text):
        """BERT subword tokenization example"""
        tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('bert-base-uncased')
        return tokenizer.tokenize(text)

print("🔷 Tokenization")
print("=" * 60)

sample_text = "I love natural language processing! It's amazing. 🚀"

# Basic tokenization
print("\n📊 Basic Word Tokenization:")
tokens_basic = TokenizationDemo.word_tokenize_basic(sample_text)
print(f"  Input: '{sample_text}'")
print(f"  Tokens: {tokens_basic}")

# NLTK tokenization
print("\n📊 NLTK Word Tokenization:")
tokens_nltk = TokenizationDemo.word_tokenize_nltk(sample_text)
print(f"  Tokens: {tokens_nltk}")

# Sentence tokenization
print("\n📊 Sentence Tokenization:")
multi_sentence = "First sentence. Second sentence! Third? Yes."
sentences = TokenizationDemo.sentence_tokenize_nltk(multi_sentence)
print(f"  Input: '{multi_sentence}'")
for i, sent in enumerate(sentences):
    print(f"  Sentence {i+1}: '{sent}'")

# BERT subword tokenization
print("\n📊 BERT Subword Tokenization:")
text_bert = "unhappiness"
tokens_bert = TokenizationDemo.subword_tokenize_bert(text_bert)
print(f"  Input: '{text_bert}'")
print(f"  BERT tokens: {tokens_bert}")
print(f"  Explanation: 'unhappiness' → ['un', '##happiness']")

# GPT-2 tokenization
print("\n📊 GPT-2 Subword Tokenization:")
tokenizer_gpt = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('gpt2')
text_gpt = "Hello, how are you?"
tokens_gpt = tokenizer_gpt.tokenize(text_gpt)
ids_gpt = tokenizer_gpt.encode(text_gpt)
print(f"  Input: '{text_gpt}'")
print(f"  Tokens: {tokens_gpt}")
print(f"  Token IDs: {ids_gpt}")
print(f"  Decoded: {tokenizer_gpt.decode(ids_gpt)}")

# Byte-Pair Encoding (BPE) demonstration
print("\n📊 Byte-Pair Encoding (BPE) Example:")
print("  BPE merges frequent character pairs to create subwords")
print("  Example: 'lowest' → ['low', 'est'] (if 'low' and 'est' are frequent)")

# Tokenization for different languages
print("\n📊 Multi-language Tokenization:")
multilingual_texts = [
    ("English", "Hello world!"),
    ("中文", "你好世界"),
    ("Emoji", "I love 💖 AI 🚀"),
    ("Mixed", "I speak English et français")
]

for lang, text in multilingual_texts:
    tokens = TokenizationDemo.word_tokenize_nltk(text)
    print(f"  {lang}: '{text}' → {tokens}")

print("\n✅ Tokenization Types:")
print("  Type        | Method                      | Example")
print("  ------------|-----------------------------|------------------")
print("  Word        | Split by spaces/punctuation | ['I', 'love', 'AI']")
print("  Sentence    | Split by .!?                | ['First.', 'Second!']")
print("  Subword     | BPE, WordPiece              | ['un', '##happy']")
print("  Character   | Split into characters       | ['I', ' ', 'l', 'o']")
print("\n💡 Tokenization in Practice:")
print("  • Modern LLMs use subword tokenization (BPE/WordPiece)")
print("  • BERT: WordPiece (## prefix for subwords)")
print("  • GPT: Byte-Pair Encoding (BPE)")
print("  • SentencePiece: Used by T5, Llama (handles all languages)")

subtopics.title: TF-IDF (Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency)
subtopics.definition: Statistical measure of word importance in documents
subtopics.explanation: TF-IDF weights words based on how important they are to a document within a corpus. TF (Term Frequency): how often a word appears in a document. IDF (Inverse Document Frequency): penalizes words that appear in many documents (common words like 'the', 'is'). Formula: TF-IDF = TF(t,d) × IDF(t) = (count of t in d) × log(N / (1 + df(t))). High TF-IDF indicates words that are frequent in a document but rare across corpus, identifying key terms. Used for text classification, search engines, document similarity, and keyword extraction.
subtopics.example: Document: 'cat cat cat dog'. Corpus: many docs. 'cat' has high TF (3) but may have high IDF if rare, 'the' has high TF but low IDF (common).
subtopics.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity
import pandas as pd

class TFIDFDemo:
    """Demonstrate TF-IDF calculations"""
    
    @staticmethod
    def manual_tfidf(corpus):
        """Manual TF-IDF calculation"""
        # Tokenize documents
        docs = [doc.lower().split() for doc in corpus]
        all_words = sorted(set(word for doc in docs for word in doc))
        N = len(docs)
        
        # Calculate document frequency
        df = {word: sum(1 for doc in docs if word in doc) for word in all_words}
        
        # Calculate IDF
        idf = {word: np.log(N / (1 + df[word])) for word in all_words}
        
        # Calculate TF-IDF for each document
        tfidf_matrix = []
        for doc in docs:
            # Term frequency
            tf = {word: doc.count(word) for word in all_words}
            # TF-IDF
            tfidf = [tf[word] * idf[word] for word in all_words]
            tfidf_matrix.append(tfidf)
        
        return np.array(tfidf_matrix), all_words, idf
    
    @staticmethod
    def sklearn_tfidf(corpus, max_features=100):
        """Sklearn TF-IDF vectorizer"""
        vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(max_features=max_features, stop_words='english')
        tfidf_matrix = vectorizer.fit_transform(corpus)
        return tfidf_matrix, vectorizer

print("\n🔷 TF-IDF (Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency)")
print("=" * 60)

# Sample corpus
corpus = [
    "The cat sat on the mat",
    "The dog sat on the mat",
    "Cats and dogs are pets",
    "I love my pet cat",
    "My dog loves to play"
]

print("\n📊 Corpus:")
for i, doc in enumerate(corpus):
    print(f"  Doc {i+1}: {doc}")

# Manual TF-IDF calculation
print("\n📊 Manual TF-IDF Calculation (First Document):")
tfidf_matrix, words, idf = TFIDFDemo.manual_tfidf(corpus)

print(f"  IDF values:")
for word in ['cat', 'the', 'dog', 'mat']:
    if word in idf:
        print(f"    '{word}': {idf[word]:.4f}")

# Show TF-IDF for first document
print(f"\n  TF-IDF for Doc 1 ('{corpus[0]}'):")
first_doc_tfidf = dict(zip(words, tfidf_matrix[0]))
for word, value in sorted(first_doc_tfidf.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:5]:
    if value > 0:
        print(f"    '{word}': {value:.4f}")

# Sklearn TF-IDF
print("\n📊 Sklearn TF-IDF Vectorizer:")
tfidf_matrix_sklearn, vectorizer = TFIDFDemo.sklearn_tfidf(corpus)
feature_names = vectorizer.get_feature_names_out()

print(f"  Feature matrix shape: {tfidf_matrix_sklearn.shape}")
print(f"  Number of features: {len(feature_names)}")
print(f"  Top features: {feature_names[:10]}")

# Document similarity using TF-IDF
print("\n📊 Document Similarity:")
cosine_sim = cosine_similarity(tfidf_matrix_sklearn)
print("  Cosine Similarity Matrix:")
for i in range(len(corpus)):
    for j in range(len(corpus)):
        if i < j:
            print(f"    Doc {i+1} vs Doc {j+1}: {cosine_sim[i][j]:.4f}")

# Keyword extraction
print("\n📊 Keyword Extraction with TF-IDF:")
for i, doc in enumerate(corpus):
    tfidf_scores = tfidf_matrix_sklearn[i].toarray()[0]
    top_indices = np.argsort(tfidf_scores)[-3:][::-1]
    keywords = [feature_names[idx] for idx in top_indices if tfidf_scores[idx] > 0]
    print(f"  Doc {i+1}: {keywords}")

# Search functionality
print("\n🔍 Search with TF-IDF:")
query = "cat pet"

# Transform query
query_vec = vectorizer.transform([query])
similarities = cosine_similarity(query_vec, tfidf_matrix_sklearn).flatten()

print(f"  Query: '{query}'")
for i, sim in enumerate(similarities):
    print(f"  Doc {i+1}: {sim:.4f} - '{corpus[i]}'")

# Visualize TF-IDF values
print("\n📊 TF-IDF vs Word Frequency:")
word_counts = {}
for doc in corpus:
    for word in doc.lower().split():
        word_counts[word] = word_counts.get(word, 0) + 1

print("  Common words (high frequency) get penalized:")
common_words = ['the', 'on', 'mat', 'my']
for word in common_words:
    if word in word_counts:
        freq = word_counts[word]
        idf_val = np.log(len(corpus) / (1 + sum(1 for doc in corpus if word in doc.lower().split())))
        print(f"    '{word}': frequency={freq}, IDF={idf_val:.4f}")

print("\n✅ TF-IDF Properties:")
print("  • TF: How frequent a word is in a document")
print("  • IDF: How rare a word is across the corpus")
print("  • TF-IDF = TF × IDF")
print("  • High TF-IDF → Important word for that document")
print("  • Common words (the, is) get low TF-IDF")
print("\n💡 Applications:")
print("  • Information Retrieval (Search engines)")
print("  • Document Similarity")
print("  • Keyword Extraction")
print("  • Text Classification")
print("  • Stop Word Detection")

print("\n📊 TF-IDF vs Other Methods:")
print("-" * 50)
print("  Method    | Pros                    | Cons")
print("  ----------|-------------------------|------------------")
print("  Bag-of-Words| Simple, interpretable | Ignores semantics")
print("  TF-IDF    | Down-weights common words | Still bag-of-words")
print("  Word2Vec  | Captures semantics     | Requires training")
print("  BERT      | Contextual embeddings   | Computationally heavy")
print("\n🔧 Practical Usage:")
print("  • Combine with classifiers (SVM, Logistic Regression)")
print("  • Use with n-grams for better context")
print("  • Apply sublinear TF (1 + log(tf))")
print("  • Use L2 normalization for document length invariance")
